recent_calls.summary: The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The user called Budget and provided their service address. They requested to speak with a representative, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Waymon Benson Junior, called to request a garage door repair covered under his home warranty. The agent collected all necessary details, confirmed the service address, and scheduled a morning callback for the next business day. The user declined additional maintenance program information but provided positive feedback about the interaction.
The call was very brief with the agent introducing herself but no further conversation occurred as the user hung up shortly after.

Dorothy called to set up a service call for her air conditioning, which is out and affecting her grandson's asthma, making it an emergency. The agent documented the issue, confirmed contact details and service address, and marked the request as urgent for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended with the agent informing Dorothy that a live representative or technician could not be dispatched immediately due to after-hours.

The user called after hours to inquire about pricing on a compressor but was informed that the office had closed early. The user expressed frustration about not receiving a callback from an earlier call and decided to try again the next day.
The user called and requested to speak with Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the requested party.

The user called to inquire about service availability at their property ZIP code 42348 in Hallsville, Kentucky. The agent informed the user that service is not available in that area and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to request a quote for work in their basement. The initial agent attempted to connect the user with a live agent who could assist further and successfully transferred the call.

The user, Deborah Small, called to inquire about pricing for a 300-ton outdoor air conditioner unit but was advised that such a size is for commercial buildings and discussed options for her 1100 square foot home. She requested a quote for a replacement outdoor unit, including new and refurbished options, and booked an appointment for June 24th at noon. The call ended with the agent attempting to transfer her to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Deborah Small, called to inquire about the installation date of her furnace and to ask questions about her air conditioning unit. She provided her address and ownership status, and expressed interest in service. The call was transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another representative. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

The user called to inquire about a specific financing link they were waiting for. The agent explained that the link comes directly from the financing provider and offered to have the team follow up, which the user accepted. The call ended with the user requesting to speak to a representative, and the agent transferred the call accordingly.

The user, Jamie Carbone, called to report that their water heater is not producing hot water and requested a technician appointment. The agent confirmed the user's details, explained the service fee, and informed them of the earliest available appointment slots on June 24th and 25th. The user declined to provide an email and expressed concern about payment timing.
The user expressed frustration about being left on hold for nine minutes during a previous call. The agent apologized and promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user, Sylvester, called and requested to speak with CG. The agent attempted to clarify the user's needs but ultimately transferred the call to another agent as requested.

The user called to schedule a service appointment related to a home warranty through American Home Shield. The agent collected all necessary details, including the dispatch number, service address, and preferred appointment time, and informed the user that Budget would follow up shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and providing feedback.

The user reported a cooling unit issue where the fan is not spinning and the house is not cooling. The agent gathered all necessary details and offered available service slots, but the user declined and decided to find another service provider. The call ended without scheduling an appointment.

The user called to get a quote for a new HVAC system to compare with an existing quote. The agent collected the user's information, confirmed the details, and offered available appointment times for an in-home quote. The user selected an appointment for June 24th at 12 PM before the call ended.

Charles Bach called to inquire about scheduling an air conditioner service under his American Home Shield home warranty, but the agent informed him that such requests must be handled through the warranty provider. The agent offered assistance with any non-warranty HVAC needs, but the user did not request further help and ended the call.

The user called to schedule seasonal HVAC maintenance and provided their details, including a corrected service address. The agent offered available appointment slots, and the user selected the earliest time on June 24, 2026, which was successfully booked.

The user called to inquire about air conditioning service for their new house at ZIP code 63005. The agent confirmed the service address and asked about the type of service needed, and the user specified air conditioning. The call ended shortly after without further details or service arrangements.
The call was very brief, with the agent initiating the conversation by asking for the service address ZIP code, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

Chris from Home Phone called Budget seeking to get in contact with Shannon or another agent. The agent, Sophie, was unable to provide direct contact information but offered to assist with service requests. The call ended with Sophie transferring Chris to another live agent for further assistance.

Jason called to schedule a replacement for his old AC unit with a Carrier unit due to a hole in the current system. The agent confirmed details including the service address and preferred appointment time, and successfully booked an appointment for June 24th between 4 and 6 PM. Jason declined to provide feedback but was satisfied with the service.

Brad called to speak with someone in the purchasing or procurement division about materials. The agent collected his contact information and assured him that the purchasing team would follow up. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.

Michelle Downey called to report a water leak from her air conditioning unit that is causing active damage to her property. The agent confirmed the details and transferred the call to a live agent for urgent assistance, noting that a technician had recently been there to address the same issue.

The user called to get an estimate for repairing their AC unit. The initial agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.
The user called Budget but quickly realized it was a mistake and apologized before ending the call. The agent responded politely and did not proceed further.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user, Derek, called to schedule a Halo Ion water treatment service and preferred Monday, Wednesday, or Friday mornings for the appointment. The agent confirmed the details and checked availability, offering several time slots. The call ended abruptly when the user was put on hold and the line disconnected before finalizing the appointment.
The call was very brief with only the agent greeting the user and no further conversation. The user hung up shortly after the greeting without any interaction.

The user called Budget seeking help with payment for an order. The agent informed the user that billing information is not accessible directly and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, greeted the user and initiated the transfer without further conversation.

The user reported receiving a call from Budget Heating and Plumbing that ended abruptly when the caller hung up. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.

John Vago called to inquire about Budget's annual maintenance agreement for air conditioning and heating. The agent provided details about the coverage and directed John to review the agreement online at justcallbudget.com. John declined further assistance and ended the call after confirming the website address.
The user requested to speak with Nicole, and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate person successfully. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

The user inquired about free furnace service calls, and the agent explained that there is a $99 fee for technician visits. The agent also mentioned a special offer for HVAC system installations but the user declined to schedule and said they would call back later.
Dan from Johnstone Supply called Budget and spoke briefly with Sophie, who then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called Budget and provided their ZIP code 63045. They requested parts, specifically mentioning DG and parts. The call ended shortly after the request without further discussion.

Thomas Wayne Faden called to inquire about an unexpected appointment scheduled for June 19th with technician Devon, which he did not remember making. The agent confirmed the appointment details and assured Thomas that his concerns would be forwarded to the team for follow-up. Thomas declined further assistance and ended the call.

The user called to confirm the appointment time for today with Alicia Gordon Smith at Saint John Cemetery. The agent verified the user's details but was unable to provide the appointment time and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to make a scheduling adjustment related to a home warranty service. The agent confirmed the nature of the request and asked for the home warranty dispatch number to proceed. The call ended shortly after without further information.
The user requested to speak with a lease agent, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate department. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
The call was very brief with only the agent greeting the user and no further interaction before the user hung up.
The user called and requested to speak with CG. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate agent.

The user called Budget and was immediately transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

The user, Cathy Butler, called to schedule routine maintenance for her air conditioning and furnace, along with a replacement of a dead thermostat. She mentioned having breathing problems and requested the earliest possible appointment, preferring phone contact for updates. The agent successfully recorded all details and assured Cathy that the team would follow up to confirm the appointment.
